title: 🎙️ Science, Wonder & the Natural World
description: Today we look at scientific discovery and the natural world — from ecology and wildlife research to astronomy and deep-sea biology. We're grounding these stories in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo: what salmon runs, boreal forest research, or species-at-risk studies tell us about the wild places we live beside. We're after stories that inspire curiosity and wonder about how the world works. - Daily themed article selection for podcast generation

_podcast.theme_description: Today we look at scientific discovery and the natural world — from ecology and wildlife research to astronomy and deep-sea biology. We're grounding these stories in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo: what salmon runs, boreal forest research, or species-at-risk studies tell us about the wild places we live beside. We're after stories that inspire curiosity and wonder about how the world works.
_podcast.theme_scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Science, Wonder & the Natural World' — scientific discovery, ecology, natural phenomena, and the curiosity-driven study of living systems. Strong fits include: wildlife biology and species research, ecology studies and biodiversity findings, watershed and salmon research relevant to BC, astronomy and space science, new species discoveries, citizen science projects, natural history stories, wildfire science and forest ecology, climate science findings (not just policy), geology, evolution, and deep-time Earth stories, and any scientific research grounded in BC or Cariboo landscapes and ecosystems. Prefer articles that convey genuine curiosity and discovery — the 'how' and 'why' of the natural world. Score 70–90 for: prehistoric life and palaeontology (ancient oceans, Cretaceous ecology, deep-time species), new species discoveries in ocean or remote environments, deep-sea biology, evolutionary biology findings, natural history stories with a strong sense of wonder, and field research grounded in BC ecosystems. Penalise (score 0–30): consumer health and pharmaceutical research without ecology or natural-world context; pure policy and advocacy news; technology business stories without a science or ecology angle; human interest and viral news without scientific substance; geopolitical and crime news. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Scientists revive a powerful antibiotic that superbugs had defeated
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Scientists have found a way to revive vancomycin, a powerful antibiotic that some dangerous bacteria have learned to resist. Instead of creating an entirely new drug, researchers paired vancomycin with a small molecule called pghi-4, which blocks a bacterial enzyme linked to resistance. The combination restored the antibiotic’s ability to kill drug-resistant E. faecium, raising hopes that similar chemical helpers could rescue other failing medicines.
items.summary: Scientists have found a way to revive vancomycin, a powerful antibiotic that some dangerous bacteria have learned to resist. Instead of creating an entirely new drug, researchers paired vancomycin with a small molecule called pghi-4, which blocks a bacterial enzyme linked to resistance. The
items._excerpt: Scientists have found a way to revive vancomycin, a powerful antibiotic that some dangerous bacteria have learned to resist. Instead of creating an entirely new drug, researchers paired vancomycin with a small molecule called pghi-4, which blocks a bacterial enzyme linked to resistance. The combination restored the antibiotic’s ability to kill drug-resistant E. faecium, raising hopes that similar chemical helpers could rescue other failing medicines.

items.title: [Atlas Obscura] Fuel Tank Orca Mural in Palmer Station, Antarctica
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

The fuel tank mural at Palmer Station is one of the most recognizable landmarks at this remote Antarctic research base. Painted on the station’s large fuel tank, the mural features a leaping orca and adds a unique piece of public art to the otherwise industrial scenery. Visible from the water as ships approach the station, it has become a familiar symbol of Palmer Station for residents and visitors alike.

The mural stands out against the surrounding Antarctic landscape and serves as a reminder that even in one of the world’s most isolated workplaces, people bring creativity and personality to their surroundings.

items.title: [Boing Boing] Germany told parents to destroy this talking doll
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

In 2014, a talking doll named My Friend Cayla was voted Innovative Toy of the Year. Three years later the German government was telling parents to destroy it. Cayla listened to a child through a hidden microphone, beamed the audio over Bluetooth to a phone app, searched the internet for an answer, and spoke back after about a one-second delay. — Read the rest

The post Germany told parents to destroy this talking doll appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [Atlas Obscura] Ike Towell’s Grave in Columbus, Texas
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Isaac "Ike" Towell was born on November 25, 1849 and died, by suicide, on February 22, 1934 at the age of 83 in Columbus, Texas.  

Ike was the city marshal of Columbus in 1885 and a lifelong "infidel".  He was known as a man of unwavering principals and had many friends near and far.  An outspoken atheist, he was recognized for his uncompromising honesty, his opposition to alcohol and tobacco, and his fearless, courageous nature. 

About a year before his death, he arranged for a headstone to be placed at his future gravesite.  A few days before he passed away, he purchased his own coffin and left detailed instructions regarding his funeral arrangements.  The service consisted of a final message written by Mr. Towell and was directed to be read by someone "not afraid that lightening would strike during the service".

Over 100 people were present at his service to hear the following message:

"Especially do I want to impress the fact upon the people that one child was born, passed through the years of youth, grew to manhood, lived a tolerably respectable life, reached old age, died and could be buried without the assistance of the clergy."

"It is my will and I so direct that my funeral be the cheapest possible. I do not hold with carrying a person from county to county for the sole purpose of burial.

"My religion consists of doing right and loving justice. I affirm that all men should tell the truth and pay their debts. I do not believe in any God, devil, ghost or savior and I have opposed tobacco, whiskey, gambling, lying and stealing practically all my life. If any one of the clergy ever calls my name after death I insist that he speak the truth about me, a thing I have never known a preacher to do about a dead disbeliever. The clergy have never been able to bribe me with the promise of a beautiful home in a fictitious heaven nor bluff my by their everlasting punishment in a hell of fire and brimstone."

Headstone Inscription:

"HERE RESTS
IKE TOWELL
AN INFIDEL WHO HAD
NO HOPE OF HEAVEN
NOR FEAR OF HELL
WAS FREE OF SUPERSTITION
TO DO RIGHT AND LOVE
JUSTICE WAS HIS RELIGION"
items.summary: Isaac "Ike" Towell was born on November 25, 1849 and died, by suicide, on February 22, 1934 at the age of 83 in Columbus, Texas. Ike was the city marshal of Columbus in 1885 and a lifelong "infidel". He was known as a man of unwavering principals and had many friends near and far. An outspoken

items.title: [Atlas Obscura] La Casa del Serpentello in Vignale Monferrato, Italy
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

What makes the Serpentello House truly special is its unique artistic character. The façade and many of its decorations are entirely covered with handcrafted ceramic tiles, each one unique and personally designed and created by the owner, Luciano Serpentello, in 1925.

This remarkable attention to detail transforms the house into a true work of art, giving it a distinctive and unforgettable atmosphere.

Non v'è arte né simmetria,
Unico pregio la fantasia.
In pochi anni di mia vita
Da me quest'opera venne compita.
Se non bella è originale
Ed è difficile trovar l'eguale.
Un contadino vi ha posto mano,
Tal Serpentello signor Luciano.

Translated by Google:

No art or symmetry is found here,
Its only merit is imagination.
In but a few years of my life,
I brought this work to completion.
If not beautiful, it is original,
And its equal is hard to find.
A farmer set his hand to it—
Signor Luciano, known as "Serpentello."

As the inscription above suggests, the Serpentello House is often regarded as a "visual encyclopedia" of rural life and the boundless imagination of its creator.

items.title: [Boing Boing] A cow escaped a slaughterhouse and hid for 40 days
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Minutes before she was to be killed at a slaughterhouse in Hopkinton, Massachusetts, a three-year-old, 1,600-pound heifer named Emily cleared the five-foot gate and ran. She stayed loose for 40 days through record snowfall in the winter of 1995, foraging in backyards and, at times, running with a herd of deer. — Read the rest

The post A cow escaped a slaughterhouse and hid for 40 days appeared first on Boing Boing.

items.title: [The Marginalian] How Not to Waste Your Life
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

“Let me not seem to have lived in vain,” the Danish astronomer Tycho Brahe whispered on his deathbed, not realizing that the astronomical tables he was leaving behind would become the portal through which Kepler arrives at the laws of planetary motion; not realizing that the measure of an unwasted life is not what outlives it but how it was lived — how much integrity and authenticity and creative vitality filled these numbered days, these unrepeatable hours. Most of us will not leave behind a revolutionary insight into the nature of the universe, but we too forget that no matter… read article

items.title: EBSCO Clinical Decisions and IHCNO Announce Exclusive Collaboration to Advance Home Care Nursing Standards
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

EBSCO Clinical Decisions and IHCNO Announce Exclusive Collaboration to Advance Home Care Nursing Standards bpeters@ebsco.com Tue, 07/21/2026 - 08:45 ~New partnership integrates IHCNO’s expert clinical validation into skills within EBSCO’s Dynamic Health™ platform, empowering home-based health care professionals with evidence-based practice~
Publish date
Tue, 07/21/2026 - 10:00

IPSWICH, Mass. — July 21, 2026 — EBSCO Clinical Decisions (EBSCO) today announced a strategic partnership with the International Home Care Nurses Organization (IHCNO), aimed at elevating clinical practice guidelines for home-based professional nursing. Through the rigorous review and validation of skills by IHCNO subject matter experts (SMEs), this exclusive collaboration infuses specialized clinical expertise into EBSCO’s Dynamic Health™ platform to equip professionals with evidence-based knowledge at the point of care.

EBSCO’s Dynamic Health tool is designed to help nurses and allied health professionals master critical skills, obtain fast answers to clinical questions, and foster a culture of evidence-based practice and critical thinking, leading to improved patient outcomes. Through this joint initiative, clinicians working in home environments gain access to IHCNO-validated knowledge through the EBSCO Dynamic Health platform, helping standardize care and elevate clinical safety.

“Home care nursing requires an incredible amount of clinical independence, as these professionals often provide care without the immediate backup of a traditional hospital or clinic environment,” said Diane Hanson, Chief Nursing Officer at EBSCO and Editor in Chief of Dynamic Health. “Our partnership with IHCNO ensures that clinicians have gold-standard, evidence-based guidance right at their fingertips. Validating these specialized home care competencies allows us to give nurses the clinical confidence they need to deliver safe, high-quality care directly in the patient’s home.”

Combining the anywhere, anytime access of EBSCO’s knowledge repository with IHCNO’s clinical expert review helps scale evidence-based care across the home health sector by focusing on three core areas:

  • Rigorous Clinical Validation: IHCNO experts will systematically review essential nursing skills within the EBSCO Dynamic Health platform's home care collection annually. This steady cadence translates to an ongoing commitment to quality clinical decision support. 
  • Targeted Home Care Competencies: The initial rollout prioritizes critical, high-stakes procedures created specifically for home settings. Users can immediately access certified guidance for topics such as ‘Identifying and Managing Hypoglycemia in Unconscious Adults in the Home’ and ‘Inserting an Indwelling Urinary Catheter in Adults in the Home’, among others. 
  • Seamless Workflow Integration & Intelligent Search: The verified resources are engineered to fit effortlessly into existing clinical workflows. Professionals can utilize EBSCO’s natural-language Dyna AI tool to instantly surface IHCNO-validated skills, balancing rapid search functionality with clinical trust. 

To learn more about IHCNO, click here. 

To learn more about EBSCO’s Dynamic Health platform, click here. 

About EBSCO Clinical Decisions 
EBSCO Information Services (EBSCO) is a leading provider of online research content and cutting-edge search technologies serving libraries, healthcare and medical institutions, corporations, and government agencies worldwide. As an AI-enabled services leader, EBSCO offers comprehensive solutions from research, acquisition management, subscription services and discovery services to clinical decision support and patient care, learning, and research and development, EBSCO provides institutions with access to content and resources to serve the information and workflow needs of their users and organizations. The EBSCO Clinical Decisions suite of products is designed to improve patient care by providing healthcare professionals with the most current, evidence-based answers to clinical questions as they arise at the point of care.  Our commitment to AI-driven innovation positions EBSCO at the forefront of the industry, enabling us to meet the evolving needs of the information services landscape. For more information, visit www.clinicaldecisions.com.

Visit our blog at Health Notes or follow us on X and LinkedIn.

About IHCNO
The International Home Care Nurses Organization (IHCNO) is the leading global professional organization dedicated to advancing home-based nursing care. Since its founding in 2009, IHCNO has united nurses, educators, researchers, administrators, and health leaders from across the world to strengthen nursing practice in homes and communities. Through international collaboration, education, research, leadership development, and advocacy, IHCNO works to improve the quality, visibility, and impact of professional home care nursing and to support nurses in delivering person-centered care where people live.

Contacts: 
Alyssa Horowitz for EBSCO 
dynamed@pancomm.com

items.title: [ScienceDaily] Ancient ferns turned Triassic Europe into a wildfire inferno
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Ancient fern savannahs may have transformed parts of Europe into a wildfire-prone inferno during the end-Triassic mass extinction. New fossil evidence suggests the ferns repeatedly regrew after fires, creating fresh fuel that helped prolong the devastation.

items.title: [The Marginalian] Borges on How to Conquer Time
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

"Time is the substance I am made of. Time is a river which sweeps me along, but I am the river; it is a tiger which destroys me, but I am the tiger; it is a fire which consumes me, but I am the fire."

items.title: [ScienceDaily] New cancer strategy could stop tumors before resistance takes hold
items.content_html:
🔭 ✏️

Researchers are applying evolutionary theory to cancer by changing treatments before tumors have time to develop resistance. Mathematical models suggest that rapid, carefully timed switches between multiple therapies could improve cure rates.
